name: Australia
welcome: G'day! Welcome to Australia — the land of kangaroos, koalas, and the Great Barrier Reef!
summary: Australia is the world's largest island — and its own continent!
It is home to some of the most unique animals on the planet.
Australia is famous for its red deserts, sunny beaches, and friendly people.

LOCATION.intro: Australia is at the bottom of the world — let's find it!

items.title: Australia in the Southern Hemisphere
narration: Australia is in the southern hemisphere, which means their summer is in December!
It is the sixth-largest country in the world by area.
Australia is completely surrounded by the Indian and Pacific Oceans.

ANIMALS.intro: Australia has animals found nowhere else on Earth!

items.title: Kangaroo
narration: Kangaroos are Australia's most famous animals and are on its national coat of arms!
Baby kangaroos, called joeys, live in their mum's warm pouch.
Kangaroos can jump 3 metres high and travel at 70 kilometres per hour!

items.title: Koala
narration: Koalas are fluffy, sleepy animals that live in eucalyptus trees!
They sleep up to 22 hours a day to save energy from their tough leaf diet.
Koalas have special fingerprints — just like humans!

items.title: Platypus
narration: The platypus is one of the strangest animals ever — it has a duck's beak, a beaver's tail, and an otter's feet!
It is one of the only mammals in the world that lays eggs.
Male platypuses have a tiny spur on their hind leg that can deliver venom!

items.title: Wombat
narration: Wombats are chunky, loveable marsupials that dig burrows underground.
A wombat's pouch faces backwards so dirt doesn't get in while it digs!
The most surprising fact: wombat poo is cube-shaped — the only animal in the world!

NATURE.intro: Australia's nature is breathtaking and one-of-a-kind!

items.title: Great Barrier Reef
narration: The Great Barrier Reef is the largest living structure on Earth!
It stretches over 2,300 kilometres along Australia's coast.
Thousands of colourful fish, turtles, and corals call it home.

items.title: Uluru
narration: Uluru is a massive red sandstone rock rising out of the Australian desert.
It glows orange, pink, and red as the sun rises and sets.
Uluru is sacred to the Aboriginal people — Australia's First Nations.

LANDMARKS.intro: Australia has some of the world's most iconic buildings!

items.title: Sydney Opera House
narration: The Sydney Opera House looks like giant white sails rising from the harbour!
It is one of the most recognisable buildings in the world.
Every year, over two million people go inside to watch concerts and shows.

FUN_FACTS.intro: Australia is full of wild and wonderful facts!

items.title: Dangerous Animals
narration: Australia is home to more venomous animals than any other country!
This includes the world's most venomous snake, spider, jellyfish, and octopus.
But do not worry — Australia is actually very safe for visitors!

items.title: Upside-Down Seasons
narration: Because Australia is in the southern hemisphere, its seasons are opposite to ours!
When it is winter in Europe, Australians are having a hot summer at the beach.
Australians celebrate Christmas in summer — with barbeques on the beach!

items.title: Ancient Culture
narration: Aboriginal Australians have lived on this land for at least 65,000 years!
They have the oldest continuous culture on Earth.
Their art, stories, and deep connection to the land are extraordinary.
